data.title: Bermuda's Lancashire Holdings annual gross premiums rise on strong underwriting results
data.description:
Overview

  • Bermuda insurance provider's gross premiums rose 5.1% to $2.26 bln in 2025

  • Profit after tax for 2025 was $293.4 mln, with ROE of 20.9%

  • Company declared $357 mln total dividends for 2025, including special dividend

Outlook

  • Lancashire expects 2026 to be more competitive but maintains confidence in rate adequacy

  • Company plans to continue investing in Lancashire US and expanding Syndicate 2010 capacity

  • Lancashire's strong capital position and credit rating upgrade support its outlook

Result Drivers

  • UNDERWRITING PROFIT - Strong underwriting results contributed to a $381.1 mln insurance service result with a discounted combined ratio of 83.7%

  • INVESTMENT RETURNS - Total investment return of 7.0% supported overall profitability, contributing $218.0 mln to results

  • CATASTROPHE LOSSES - Despite $277.0 mln in net losses from catastrophe events, including California wildfires, profit after tax was $293.4 mln

data.title: Morgan Stanley does not expect ECB interest rate cuts in 2026
data.description:
Morgan Stanley said on Thursday that it expects the European Central Bank to keep interest rates steady through 2026, citing persistent inflation due to the conflict in the Middle East.

The U.S. bank had previously forecast two rate cuts in June and September this year, but now sees the ECB delivering those reductions in 2027 instead.

data.title: Endeavour Mining's 2025 cash flow rises on high gold prices
data.description:
Overview

  • Gold miner's FY-2025 adjusted EBITDA rose 75%, with adjusted net earnings up 244%

  • Record FY-2025 free cash flow of $1.2 bln, up 269% from FY-2024

  • Company returned $435 mln to shareholders, 93% above minimum commitment

Outlook

  • Endeavour forecasts FY-2026 production of 1,090-1,265 koz at AISC of $1,600-$1,800/oz

  • Company targets minimum $1 bln dividend over 2026-2028, potentially doubling at current gold prices

  • Endeavour aims to discover 12-15 Moz of resources by 2030 at <$40/oz discovery cost

Result Drivers

  • HIGHER GOLD PRICES - Increased realised gold prices significantly boosted margins and cash flows

  • PRODUCTION INCREASE - Full-year commercial production from Sabodala-Massawa BIOX plant and Lafigué mine contributed to higher output

  • EXPLORATION SUCCESS - Increased reserves and resources at Assafou and other sites enhanced growth prospects

data.title: Swedish headline inflation 1.7% y/y in February, flash data shows
data.description:
Swedish consumer prices, measured with a fixed interest rate (CPIF), were up 1.7% year-on-year in February, a flash estimate released by the statistics office showed on Thursday.

Analysts had forecast headline inflation of 1.8%, according to a Reuters poll.

The statistics office said food price inflation had slowed while housing costs were rising. It gave no further details.

Inflation excluding volatile energy costs, a measure the Riksbank looks closely at, was 1.4% on an annual basis. That was also one tenth of a percentage point below analysts' forecasts.

In January, Riksbank left its policy rate unchanged at 1.75% and said it did not expect to make any adjustment for some time to come.

Analysts have pointed to the risk of inflation undershooting the Riksbank's 2.0% target this year and several have said that a rate cut was not off the table.

But the attacks on Iran by the U.S. and Israel have made the outlook much harder to judge.

data.title: China, Hong Kong stocks rebound as Beijing vows tech investment
data.description:
China and Hong Kong stocks tracked rebounds in Asian markets on Thursday, as anxiety over the Middle East war was countered by investors' emboldened bets in Chinese tech shares after Beijing vowed to deepen investment in innovation.

But Chinese bonds and the yuan were little moved as China set its 2026 economic growth target at 4.5%-5% - lower than last year and signalled no appetite for big fiscal or monetary stimulus.

The policies unveiled at the opening session of China's parliament "are generally mild, without aggressive moves," said Yuan Yuwei, fund manager at Trinity Synergy Investments in Hong Kong.

He added that while global market rebounds inspired China's gains, uncertainty around the Iran conflict will continue to weigh on sentiment.

China's blue-chip CSI300 Index closed up 1%, and the Shanghai Composite Index added about 0.6%.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng , which hit a two-month low on Wednesday, climbed as much as 1.9% before easing much of the gains in the afternoon.

China's 10-year government bond prices (CFTc1) were little changed. The yuan traded flat at 6.8956 per dollar at 0705 GMT.

Asian shares rallied on Thursday, pointing to a tentative recovery in risk appetite that has been hammered by the escalating war in the Middle East.

BEIJING VOWS TO EXPAND TECH INVESTMENT

China on Thursday vowed to deepen investment in high-tech industries and scientific innovation, framing them as essential to bolstering national security and self-reliance amid rising geopolitical tensions and a rivalry with the U.S.

Shares of Chinese artificial intelligence firms , chipmakers (.STARCHIP) and biotech companies (.CSI931440) jumped after the government pledged support to these emerging strategic industries (.CSI000891).

"The policy signal is loud and clear," said Liu Chenjie, chief economist at Upright Asset Management.

"China will focus on technology advancement" and expand domestic demand.

On Thursday, China said it would build a "childbirth-friendly society" in the next five years, pledging to address concerns over employment, education, medical care, health and income.

But reflecting investor scepticism over whether Beijing will succeed in boosting domestic demand, China's consumption-related stocks (.CSI931068) fell.

Elsewhere, defence stocks (.CSI399959) gained 0.4% underperforming the broader market, after China announced a 7% increase in defence spending in 2026.

Property shares (.CSI000948) also lost ground in the absence of strong stimulus measures to prop up the struggling industry.

Marco Sun, chief financial market analyst at MUFG (China), said that policy signals imply that monetary policy will focus on "supporting 'new economy' sectors, particularly AI and related industries, through lower financing costs and targeted credit support, rather than broad-based stimulus."

data.title: Japan futures drift lower on expectations of supply picking up
data.description:
By Ruth Chai

  • Japanese rubber futures declined on Thursday, as the market expected supply to start picking up before peak production season in April.

  • The Osaka Exchange (OSE) rubber contract for August delivery , fell 1.5 yen, or 0.4%, to 371.9 yen ($2.37) per kg.

  • The rubber contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) for May delivery fell 140 yuan, or 0.84%, to 16,555 yuan ($2,401.22) per metric ton.

  • The most active April butadiene rubber contract on the SHFE (SHBRv1) rose 515 yuan, or 3.76%, to 14,210 yuan per metric ton.

  • March marks a turning point for rubber harvesting, bridging the wintering season in February and peak production season in April. As such, expectations of supply picking up are starting to be priced into the market, a note from Baocheng Futures said.

  • The price of Thailand's benchmark export-grade smoked rubber sheet (RSS3) (RUB-RSS3C-BKK) dipped below 80 baht per kg from March 2-4, but remained elevated after rising for weeks on softer Thai supply.

  • However, pledges of policy stimulus in top consumer China, as well as declining rubber production in second-largest producer Indonesia helped curb downside.

  • Investors also eyed the annual parliamentary meeting in top consumer China on the day and the release of its 15th five-year plan, for signals on economic stimulus and domestic demand.

  • The world's biggest rubber consumer pledged to trim its growth prospects to rebalance its economy and boost domestic consumption, as it upgrades its industrial complex.

  • Indonesia, a major global producer of natural rubber, saw a 0.7% year-on-year drop in production to 2.12 million tons in 2025, according to Chinese commodity data provider SunSirs.

  • The front-month rubber contract on the Singapore Exchange's SICOM platform for April delivery last traded flat at 194.4 U.S. cents per kg as of 0705 GMT.

($1 = 157.0700 yen)

($1 = 6.8944 yuan)

data.title: British homebuilder Taylor Wimpey sees profit dip in 2026
data.description:
British homebuilder Taylor Wimpey on Thursday forecast lower profit for 2026, as softer pricing and elevated costs weigh on its business.

The company said it expects 2026 adjusted operating profit to be around 400 million pounds ($533.04 million), compared with 420.6 million pounds reported in the prior year.

data.title: UK's Harbour Energy 2025 revenue up on record production
data.description:
Overview

  • UK oil and gas producer's 2025 revenue rose, driven by record production levels

  • Company's free cash flow increased significantly to $1.1 bln

Outlook

  • Company forecasts 2026 free cash flow at c.$0.6 bln
